report_name: D1R Ransomware Claims Supply Chain Attack on Bosch via Synopsys

threat_actors.name: D1R
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that has claimed responsibility for a supply chain attack against Bosch by leveraging information stolen from Synopsys. The group is known for targeting large corporations and taunting victims on its leak site.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft and financial extortion via ransomware.

attack_patterns.name: Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: The D1R group compromised Bosch by using information obtained from a prior breach at its supplier, Synopsys.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: It is assessed as highly likely that the D1R group used credentials stolen from Synopsys to gain access to Bosch's systems.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The D1R group stole proprietary data from both Bosch and Synopsys, specifically mentioning the 'Bosch CAN module implementation' documents.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The final stage of the attack involved the deployment of D1R ransomware to encrypt data for extortion.

malware.name: D1R Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the D1R group to encrypt victim files as part of their double-extortion scheme.

identities.name: Bosch
identities.description: A German multinational engineering and technology company that was the downstream victim of a supply chain attack by the D1R ransomware group.

identities.name: Synopsys
identities.description: A U.S.-based electronic design automation (EDA) company that was compromised by the D1R group, with the stolen information being used to attack its partner, Bosch.

infrastructure.name: D1R Leak Site
infrastructure.description: The dark web leak site used by the D1R ransomware group to post claims of their attacks on Bosch and Synopsys and to publish stolen data.

indicators.name: Anomalous CAN Module Design File Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Access to CAN Module Design Files
id: 3b9c1a8f-5d2e-4b1c-8a7f-9e3d2c1b0a9e
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects unusual access to file paths related to CAN module implementation designs. This could indicate theft of critical intellectual property, as claimed by the D1R ransomware group in the Bosch/Synopsys supply chain attack.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/d1r-ransomware-group-compromises-bosch/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-14
tags:
  - attack.t1213
  - attack.collection
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
level: high
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate access by R&D personnel or automated design tools.
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '/designs/CAN_module/'
      - '\designs\CAN_module\'
  filter_legit_process:
    Image|endswith:
      - 'eda_tool.exe' # Placeholder for legitimate EDA tools
      - 'design_compiler.exe' # Placeholder
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_process
indicators.description: Detects unusual access to file paths containing critical intellectual property like 'CAN module implementation', based on hunting hints from the D1R attack on Bosch.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous CAN Module Design File Access
